destinations.evidence: The registry reader treats a job as duplicate-blocking only when it exists and expiryBlock is strictly greater than the current block.
The payload builder duplicate check uses the expiry-aware registry function before skipping a scheduled async augmentation.
The verifier rejects duplicate commitments only when the existing job is still active, matching the builder boundary.

equivalence_argument: Builder and verifier duplicate checks share the expiry-aware AsyncJobTracker reader, so expired jobs are treated as non-existent while active jobs reject/skip duplicates.
rationale: The merge adds job_exists_and_active(), uses expiryBlock to distinguish active from expired jobs, and updates both payload builder and verifier duplicate-commitment checks to call it instead of job_exists(). No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building and block verification must reject a new async commitment only when AsyncJobTracker already contains an active job for the origin transaction; expired jobs with expiryBlock <= current block must be treated as non-existent so a new commitment may overwrite them.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate AsyncJobTracker storage with a nonzero jobExecutor and expiryBlock greater than the block being built and assert builder skips and verifier rejects a duplicate commitment. Repeat with expiryBlock equal to or less than the current block and assert both builder and verifier allow the new commitment path.
summary: Duplicate async job checks are expiry-aware in both builder and verifier.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate AsyncJobTracker with expiryBlock equal to current block and greater than current block; assert builder/verifier allow the expired case and skip/reject the active case.
title: Duplicate async jobs are expiry-aware
